pages.text: 107 -16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   Tuesday,   June   9,   2015,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of   West  Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   passed   Resolution   156-15   which   authorized   the   commencement   of   a  declaratory   judgment   action   consistent   with   the   transitional   process   established   in   the   New  Jersey   Supreme   Court   In   the   Matter   of   the   Adoption   of   N.J.A.C.   5:96   and   5:97   by   the   New  Jersey   Council   on   Affordable   Housing ,   221   N.J.   1   (2015)   (the   “ Affordable   Housing   Decision ” )   to  obtain judgment approving of the Township ’ s affordable housing plan ; and  WHEREAS,   Resolution   156-15   also   authorized   the   execution   of   a   Shared   Services  Agreement   and   payment   of   $2,000   for   the   retention   of   expert   witness   who   would   provide   reports  and   testimony,   if   necessary,   on   a   behalf   of   a   consortium   of   municipalities   filing   similar  declaratory judgment actions; and  WHEREAS ,   the   consortium   of   municipalities   has   retained   Econsult   Solutions,   Inc.  ( “ Econsult ” )   who   provided   various   reports   submitted   to   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey   in   the  pending   declaratory   judgment   actions   proposing   fair   share   housing   obligations   and   other   issues  arising in the various declaratory judgment actions; and  WHEREAS,   the   consortium   of   municipalities   requires   additional   funds   to   further   the  retention   of   the retained expert witnesses to provide deposition and trial testimony;   and  WHEREAS,   the   consortium   of   municipalities   has   requested   that   each   municipality   pay  an additional $2,000 to continue the retention of Econsult.

2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   the   Township   is   hereby   authorized   to   submit   an  additional $2,000 payment to further the retention of Econsult ; and be it further  RESOLVED   this   resolution   shall   be   published   and   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  I hereby certify funds are available from:  Account No.  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO  4830-1647-4416, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING WEST ORANGE RECREATION FIREWORKS 2016

pages.text: 109-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   an   application   has   been   submitted   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange  Recreation   Department,   which   wishes   to   sponsor   the   Township ’ s   annual   Fourth   of   July  fireworks   exhibition   at   Degnan   Field/West   Orange   High   School   on   J uly   4,   2016   (Rain   Date:   July  9 , 2016 ); and  WHEREAS,   all   of   the   necessary   documentation   has   been   submitted   to   the   Township  Clerk,   on   an   Application   for   Permission   to   Conduct   Fireworks   Display   (the   “ Application ” ),   as  required by State and local law; and  WHEREAS,   the   Application   indicates   that   Pyro   Engineering,   Inc.,   of   999   South   Oyster  Bay   Road,   Suite   111,   Bethpage,   NY   11714,   will   provide   and   supervise   the   fireworks   display;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Application   has   been   reviewed   and   approved   by   the   Township   Police  Chief and Fire Official;  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Recreation   Department   be  and   is   hereby   granted   permission   to   conduct   a   f ireworks   display   on   July   4,   2016   (Rain   Dat e:  July   9 ,   2016 );   subject   to   the   directives   of   the   Fire   Official   and   Police   Chief,   or   their   designees;  and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   a  contract   between   the   Township   of   West   Orange   and   Pyro   Engineering,   Inc.   for   the   Fourth   of  July fireworks exhibition.  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  4830-9703-4787, v. 1

documents.title: AUTHORIZING MONTCLAIR YMCA USE OF DUENKEL POOL 2016

pages.text: 110-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   owns   and   maintains   an  outdoor   pool   facility,   which   includes   locker   room   facilities ,   at   the   Ginny   Duenkel   Pool   Complex,  located at 60 ½ Cherry Street   in   the Township (the   “ Pool Facilities ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   YMCA   has   for   many   years   used   the   Pool   Facilities   during  limited hours for   youth   swim practices   and for competitive swimming programs ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   YMCA   has   once   again   requested   to   use   the   Pool   Facilities   for  youth   swim   practices   during   the   summer   201 6   season,   generally   between   the   hours   of   6:00  A.M.   and   7:45   A.M.   on   weekdays   from   approximately   May   1 ,   201 6   through   August   31,   201 6 ;  and  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   YMCA   has   agreed   to   use   the   Pool   Facilities   in   exchange   for  the   payment   of   an   hourly   rental   fee,   plus   payment   of   various   costs   and   expenses,   pursuant   to  other   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   License   Agreement   annexed   hereto   as   Attachment  “ A ” ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   YMCA   has   agreed   that   its   use   of   the   Pool   Facilities   will   not  conflict with the Township ’ s uses; and  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   YMCA   has   agreed   to   comply   with   all   pool   regulations   and   all  applicable laws;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   enter   into   the  annexed   License   Agreement   for   the   Montclair   YMCA ’ s   limited   use   of   Pool   Facilities   and   the  Municipal Clerk shall be authorized to attest to   the Mayor ’ s signature ; and   be it   further

RESOLVED,   that   the   entry   of   the   License   Agreement   shall   be   subject   to   the   Licensee ’ s  providing   proof   of   adequate   insurance   with   the   Township   named   as   an   additional   insured,  subject to   approval of the Law Department, prior to the use of the Pool Facilities.  Karen J . Carnevale , R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  4822-9875-0256, v. 1

110-16  Exhibit   “ A ”  LICENSE AGREEMENT FOR  LIMITED USE OF POOL FACILITIES  THIS   AGREEMENT,   effective   May   ___,   2016   upon   full   execution   by   all  parties, between:  Township of West Orange, as Licensor ("Township" or "Licensor")  and  The Montclair YMCA, as Licensee ("Montclair Y" or "Licensee")  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   Y   runs   a   competitive   swimming   program   in   the   summer   and  requires pool time for the same; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   owns   a   certain   outdoor   pool   and   surrounding   faci lity   in   the  Township located at 60½ Cherry Street, West Orange, New Jersey ("Pool Facility").  WHEREAS,   the   Montclair   Y   is   desirous   of   using   West   Orange ’ s   outdoor   swimming  pool and changing room facilities;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   mu tual   promises   and   covenants   contained  herein , the parties hereto agree as follows:

2  1.   License   Date   and   Effective   Date   of   Agreement .   This   License   is   made   with  an   effective   date   of   May   1,   2016   ("Effective   Date").   This   Agreement   shall   not   be   effective  until   the   Township   receives   a   copy   of   this   Agreement   executed   by   the   President   of   the   Board  of   Trustees   or   Executive   Director   of   Licensee   and   the   required   insurance   certificate   approved  by   the   Township   Attorney   along   with   a   Resolution   evidencing   approval   by   the   Licensor's  Township Council.  2.   Description   of   Agreement   Premises .   The   premises   licensed   by   Township   to  Licensee   includes   the   pool   and   locker   rooms   located   at   60   ½   Cherry   Street   in   the   Township  of West Orange, New Jersey ("Premises").  3.   Term .   The   term   of   this   License   shall   be   for   four   (4)   months,   beginning   on  May   1,   2016   through   August   31,   2016.   The   Director   of   Recreation   reserves   the   right   to  terminate this contract at any time at his/her sole discretion.  4.   Licensee's   Use .   Licensee   shall   use   the   Premi ses   for   swim   practices   and   not  for   any   other   use.   The   hours   of   use   will   be   6:00   a.m.   until   approximately   7:45   a.m.   Monday  through   Friday   beginning   no   earlier   than   May   1   and   ending   no   later   than   August   31   Assigned  days   and   hours   are   subject   to   change   durin g   the   period   of   this   agreement   if   West   Orange  activities   mandate   such   change.   In   the   event   of   a   conflict   with   a   Township   activity   where  pool   and   changing   room   use   is   required,   the   Township   shall   contact   the   Senior   Aquatics  Director   of   the   Montclair   Y   and   n otify   the   Licensee   of   the   unavailability   of   the   premises   as  soon as the conflict is evident. No alternate arrangements need be made.  5.   Fee   and   Insurance .   A   fee   shall   be   payable   in   the   amount   of   $65.00   per   hour  of   actual   use.   If   the   Township   is   required   t o   hire   a   manager   or   other   authorized   individual,  the   Licensee   shall   pay   those   additional   expenses.   Licensee   agrees   to   maintain   public   liability  insurance   naming   itself   and   Township   as   named   insured   in   an   amount   not   less   than   one  million   ($1,000,000)   dolla rs   per   occurrence   and   two   million   ($2,000,000)   dollars   in   the  aggregate.   The   Montclair   Y   shall   carry   liability   insurance   with   a   minimum   combined   single  limit   of   One   Million   ($1,000,000.00)   dollars   for   bodily   injury   and   property   damage.   The  Montclair   Y   sha ll   provide   the   original   of   a   Certificate   of   Insurance   to   this   effect   upon   signing  this   agreement.   Further,   the   Montclair   Y   represents   that   all   participants   and   staff   have  additional   medical   insurance   and   emergency   room   coverage   under   a   policy   provided   thro ugh  United   States   Swimming   (liability   amount   of   $10,000,000.00)   and   will   provide   a   copy   of   that  documentation   included   with   this   signed   agreement.   The   policy   expiration   dates   shall   be   no  earlier than August 31, 2015.  6.   Indemnification .   Licensee   shall   inde mnify   and   hold   harmless   Licensor  including   attorneys   fees   and   expenses   from   any   claims   arising   from   Licensee's   use   of   the  Premises.  7.   Usage  .   Licensee   shall   comply   with   all   state   and   local   laws,   regulations,   codes  and   ordinances.   In   addition,   Licensee   shal l   comply   with   all   Township   pool   rules   as   shall   be  promulgated   from   time   to   time   by   the   Township.   Licensee   hereby   acknowledges   receipt   and  full understanding of all current pool rules.

8.   Hours   of   Licensee   Use .   Licensee   shall   be   permitted   to   use   the   Premise s  during   the   hours   of   6:00   a.m.   until   7:45   a.m.   Monday   through   Friday   during   the   term   set   forth  above.   Any   other   hours   of   use   shall   be   subject   to   prior   written   request   and   approval   by   the  Township   in   its   sole   discretion.   Licensee   may   not   use   the   Premises   at   any   time   without   the  Licensor ’ s authorized representative present.  9.   Licensee's   Care .   Licensee   will   commit   no   act   of   waste,   will   take   good   care   of  the   Premises,   and   will   comply   with   all   laws,   regulations,   rules,   and   orders   of   any   federal,  state,   or   local   government   agencies   or   departments.   Licensee   will   do   nothing   that   may  potentially i ncrease the cost of Township's fire or public liability insurance.  10.   Staffing/Supervision   &   Safety:   Licensee   shall   provide   a   certified   swim  coach   (USAS)   for   all   competitive   swimming   activities   and   shall   also   provide   a   certified   pool  manager   (CPO)   to   properly   open   the   pool   and   assure   its   safety   for   participants.   It   shall   be   the  responsibility   of   the   Licen see   to   ensure   the   safety   of   its   staff   and   all   participants   and   to   ensure  that   all   staff   and   participants   are   properly   trained   in   safety   and   health   procedures   and  concerns.   The   Licensee   shall   immediately   report   any   incidents   to   West   Orange   in   writing,  shall   provide   the   Township   with   a   written   incident   report,   and   shall   fully   cooperate   with   the  Township   in   all   regards.   The   Licensee   shall   assume   all   liabilities   of   injuries   resulting   from  swim team related activities. All of Licensee ’ s

4  employees ,   agents   and   representatives   shall   be   free   of   any   criminal   charge   or   conviction  having any impact upon their ability to work with or be in the vicinity of m inors.  11.   Lifeguard:   A   fully   certified   lifeguard   shall   be   present   at   all   times.   The  Licensee   will   pay   all   costs   for   providing   the   lifeguard   and   shall   reimburse   West   Orange  for the utilization of a West Orange staff lifeguard.  12.   Repairs .   Township   will   make   all   necessary   repairs   to   the   Premises   at  Township's   expense   except   when   the   repairs   are   caused   by   the   misuse   or   neglect   by  Licensee   or   by   persons   under   Licensee's   control   or   on   the   Premises   at   Licensee's  invitation,   in   which   even,   Lic ensee   shall   make   those   repairs   at   Licensee's   own   expense.  Licensee   shall   surrender   the   Premises   in   the   same   condition   as   they   were   at   beginning   of  the term, reasonable wear and tear excepted.  13.   Improvements .   Licensee   shall   not   cause   any   improvements,   re novations  or   other   work   to   be   done   at   the   Premises.   All   of   Licensee's   personal   property   shall   be  removed   by   Licensee   on   a   daily   basis   upon   the   completion   of   Licensee's   hours   of   use  hereunder.  14.   Abandoned   Property .   All   of   Licensee's   property   remaining   on   the  Premises shall be deemed abandoned and the sole property of the Township.  15.   Payment.   An   initial   payment   of   $2,000   shall   be   due   to   the   Township   by  no   later   than   May   31,   2016.   The   remaining   balance,   the   amount   of   which   will   be  presented   to   the   Licen see   by   the   Township   by   no   later   than   September   15,   2016,   shall   be  due to the Township by no later than October 15, 2016.  16.   Services.   The   Township   shall   provide   no   services   for   Licensee's   use  except   as   specifically   provided   herein.   Licensee   agrees   to   pla ce   all   garbage   in   a  designated   area.   Township   agrees   to   maintain   the   electrical,   plumbing,   HVAC   and  structural systems on the Premises.  17.   Damage   to   Building .   If   the   Premises   are   damaged   by   fire   or   any   other  cause, then Township may, give Licensee notice o f election to terminate this Agreement.  18.   Township   May   Terminate .   If   Licensee   defaults   in   the   performance   of  any   covenant   or   condition   of   this   Agreement,   Township   may,   on   written   notice   to  Licensee terminate this Agreement.  19.   Notices .   Any   notice   by   eithe r   party   to   the   other   shall   be   in   writing   and  shall   be   deemed   to   be   properly   given   only   if   delivered   personally,   telecopied ,   or   mailed  by   registered   or   certified   mail,   return   receipt   requested,   addressed   (a)   if   to   Licensee,   at   the  above   indicated   location;   (b)   if   to   Township,   at   Township ’ s   address   set   out   in   this  Agreement;   or   (c)   at   such   addresses   as   Licensee   or   Township   from   time   to   time   may

5  designate   in   writing.   Notice   shall   be   deemed   to   have   been   given   upon   delivery   if  delivered personally, and if maile d, upon the third day after the date of mailing.  20.   Township ’ s   Right   to   Inspect   Premises .   Township   may   enter   the  premises   at   any   time   without   notice   to   Licensee   to   inspect   the   premises   or   make   those  repairs,   replacements,   and   additions   to   the   Premises   as   To wnship   deems   necessary   or  desirable.   Licensee   shall   have   no   claim   or   cause   of   action   against   Township   solely   for  entering the premises in accordance with the Paragraph.  21.   Interruption   of   Services   or   of   Occupancy .   Interruption   or   curtailment   of  any   service   maintained   on   the   Premises   if   caused   by   strikes,   mechanical   difficulties,   or  any   other   causes   beyond   Township ’ s   control   shall   not   entitle   Licensee   to   any   claim  against   Township   or   to   any   abatement   in   rent,   nor   shall   they   constitute   constructive   or  partial   eviction.  22.   Elements   of   Construction   Eviction .   Licensee   shall   not   be   entitled   to  claim a constructive eviction from the Premises.  23.   No   Representations .   Neither   party   has   made   any   representations   or  promises,   other   than   those   contained   in   this   Agreement   or   in   some   further   writing   signed  by the party making the representation or promise.  24.   Covenant   of   Quiet   Enjoyment .   Township   covenants   that   as   long   as  Licensee   pays   the   rent   and   any   additional   rent   required   under   this   Agreement   and  performs   Licensee ’ s   covena nts,   Licensee   shall   peaceably   and   quietly   have,   hold,   and  enjoy the premises for the term provided, subject to the provisions of this Agreement.  25.   Waiver   of   Jury   Trial .   To   the   extent   waiver   is   permitted   by   law,   the  parties   waive   trial   by   jury   in   any   action   or   proceeding   brought   in   connection   with   this  Agreement or the premises.  26.   Captions .   The   captions   in   this   Agreement   are   included   for   convenience  only   and   shall   not   be   taken   into   consideration   in   any   construction   or   interpretation   of   this  Agreement or any   of its provisions.  27.   No   Assignment .   The   Licensee   is   not   permitted   to   assign   sublet   or   transfer  any aspect of this Agreement, in whole or in part, to any entity or person.  28.   Agreement   Binding   on   Successors,   Representatives,   and   Assigns .   The  provisions   of   th is   Agreement   shall   apply   to,   bind,   and   inure   to   the   benefit   of   Township  and Licensee, their successors, legal representatives, and assigns.  29.   Entire   Agreement:   This   Agreement   may   not   be   modified   except   by  written agreement signed by both of the parties he reto.

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING PLACEMENT OF LIEN AT 19 ELMWOOD

pages.text: 111-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   issued   a   Summons   bearing  Complaint   No.   SC- 035022   for   failure   to   maintain   the   exterior   premises   of   certain   real   property  located at   19 Elmwood Avenue , West Orange, New Jersey   (the   “ Property ” )   on   May 7, 2015 ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Property   was   examined   by   Forester   John   Linson ,   who   determined   that   a  tree   on   the   Property   was   in   dead   and   hazardous   condition,   and   recommended   that   the   tree   be  removed for safety immediately ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   owner   of   the   Property   (the   “ Owner ” )   was   issued   the   Summons   and  failed to remove the dead tree from the Property; and  WHEREAS ,   the   West   Orange   Municipal   Court   (the   “ Court ” )   entered   an   Order   on  January   20,   2016   (the   “ January   20   Order ” ),   permitting   the   Township   or   a   third-party   vendor   to  enter onto the Property to remove the hazardous tree; and  WHEREAS ,   the   January   20   Order   was   served   on   the   Owner   by   posting   the   January   20  Order on the door of the Property, certified mail, and first class mail; and  WHEREAS ,   the   dead   tree   was   removed   from   the   Property   by   Dujets   Tree   Experts,   Inc.,  a third-party contractor hired by the Township to remove the hazardous tree; and  WHEREAS ,   the   itemized   bill   for   the   removal   of   the   tree,   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed  hereto   as   Exhibit   A ,   reflects   that   the   total   cost   of   the   services   associated   with   the   removal   of  the tree was $ 1,875 .00; and  WHEREAS ,   the   January   20   Order   permitted   the   Township   to   seek   reimbursement   for  the expenses incurred to abate the violation;   and  WHEREAS ,   on   March   24,   2016 ,   the   Township   requested   reimbursement   by   the   Owner  for the expenses incurred to abate the violation ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Owner   failed   to   reimburse   the   Township   for   the   expenses   incurred   to  abate the violation;   and

111-16  May 3, 2016  2  WHEREAS ,   the   January   20   Order   permits   the   Township   to   seek   an   O rder   allowing   the  claim to be recorded as a lien on the Property;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Court   entered   an   Order   on   April   13,   2016   permitting   the   Township   to  record   a   lien   on   the   Property,   and   to   collect   statutorily   authorized   interest   on   that   lien   as   set   forth  in   N.J.S.A.   54:4-67;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   a   lien   in   the   amount   of   $ 1,875 .00   shall   be   placed   against   19   Elmwood  Avenue ,   West   Orange   for   the   cost   of   services   required   to   remove   the   hazardous   tree   on   the  Property; and be it further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  4834-6396-6000, v. 1
111-16  Exhibit A

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING PLACEMENT OF LIEN AT 3 WILDWOOD

pages.text: 112-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   issued   a   Summons   bearing  Complaint   No.   SC- 035023   for   failure   to   maintain   the   exterior   premises   of   certain   real   property  located at   3 Wildwood   Avenue , West Orange, New Jersey   (the   “ Property ” ) ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Property   was   examined   by   Forester   John   Linson ,   who   determined   that  six   (6)   ash   trees   on   the   Property   were   in   dead   and   hazardous   condition   (the   “ Trees ” ) ,   and  recommended   that the   Trees   be removed for safety immediately ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   owner   of   the   Property   (the   “ Owner ” )   was   issued   the   Summons   and  failed to remove the dead   Trees   from the Property; and  WHEREAS ,   the   West   Orange   Municipal   Court   (the   “ Court ” )   entered   a   Consent   Order   on  March   3 ,   2016   (the   “ March   3   Consent   Order ” ),   permitting   the   Township   or   a   third-party   vendor   to  enter onto the Property to remove the hazardous   Trees ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   March   3   Consent   Order   was   served   on   the   Owner   via   first   class   mail;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Trees   w ere   removed   from   the   Property   by   Montclair   Tree   Experts,   Inc.,  a third-party contractor hired by the Township to remove the   Trees ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   itemized   bill   for   the   removal   of   the   Trees ,   a   copy   of   which   is   annexed  hereto   as   Exhibit   A ,   reflects   that   the   total   cost   of   the   services   associated   with   the   removal   of  the   Trees   was $ 5,800 .00; and  WHEREAS ,   the   March   3   Consent   Order   permitted   the   Township   to   seek   reimbursement  for the expenses incurred to abate the violation;   and  WHEREAS ,   on   April   11 ,   2016 ,   the   Township   requested   reimbursement   by   the   Owner   for  the expenses incurred to abate the violation ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Owner   failed   to   reimburse   the   Township   for   the   expenses   incurred   to  abate the violation;   and

112-16  May 3, 2016  2  WHEREAS ,   the   March   3   Consent   Order   permit s   the   Township   to   record   a   lien   on   the  Property,   and   to   collect   statutorily   authorized   interest   on   that   lien   as   set   forth   in   N.J.S.A.   54:4-  67;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   a   lien   in   the   amount   of   $ 5,800 .00   shall   be   placed   against   3   Wildwood   Avenue ,  West   Orange   for   the   cost   of   services   required   to   remove   the   Trees   on   the   Property;   and   be   it  further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  4843-5062-8401, v. 1
112-16  Exhibit A

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING COMPREHENVISE REVIEW AND OVERHAUL OF TOWNSHIPS EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK

pages.text: 113-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   revise   and  update   its   Employee   Manual   of   Policies   and   Procedures   (the   “ Employee   Manual ” )   to   insure   that  the   Employee   Manual   reflects   all   current   Township   policies,   practices   and   procedures   and   to  further   insure   that   the   Township ’ s   policies,   practices   and   procedures   are   consistent   and   in  accordance with all current law s ; and  WHEREAS,   the   law   firm   Fox   Rothschild,   LLP   ( “ Special   Counsel ” )   has   already   been  retained as Labor Counsel for the Township for the year 2016; and  WHEREAS,   Counsel   has   agreed   to   perform   a   comprehensive   review   of   the   Township ’ s  Employee   Manual   at   the   hourly   rate   set   forth   in   its   Agreement   for   Legal   Services   with   the  Township,   with   a   cap   on   total   fees   in   performing   its   r eview   of   the   Employee   Manual   at   $2,000 ;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Law   Department   recommends   authorizing   Counsel   to   p erform   the  comprehensive review of the Employee Manual.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Township   is   hereby   authorized   to   instruct   Special   Counsel   to   commence   a  comprehensive   review   of   the   Township ’ s   Employee   Manual   at   an   hourly   rate   of   $175   with   a   cap  on   total   fees at $2,000   related to this   engagement ; and it is further

113-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   published   and   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  office in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF SERVICE CONTRACT WITH ABBRY SECUIRTY FOR MUNICIPAL COURT SE

pages.text: 118-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   in   need   of   a   company   to   staff   the   security  post   outside the Municipal Courtroom when court is in session; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   is   familiar   with   and   has   used   the   services   of   Abbry   Security  LLC   since   the   year   2007   for   una rm ed   security   services   and   has   been   satisfied   with   the   services  they   provide the Township's Municipal Court; and  WHEREAS,   Abbry   Security   LLC   is   a   division   of   First   Community   Development   Corp .   of  NJ   who is an authorized   and registered   Shelter Workshop; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   can   secure   a   contract   with   Abbry   Security   LLC   without   the  public   advertisement of bids pursuant to   N . J.S.A.   40A:11-5(n); and  WHEREAS,   Abbry   Security   LLC   has   proposed   to   maintain   the   existing   hourly   rate   of  $1 5.00   and   the   duration   of   this   contract   will   be   for   24   months   consistent   with   the   terms   of   the  service contract annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A. ”  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Township   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   exercise   the  service   contract   with   Abbry   Security   LLC,   187   Cortlandt   Street,   Belleville,   NJ   07109 ,   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ A ”   for th e above mentioned rate and term; and it is further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   resolution   shall   be   published   and   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s  office in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted :   May 3, 2016  I hereby certify funds are available from:   01-2010-00-5003095  Not To Exceed $34.000.00   Account No.  JOHN O. GROSS, CFO  4821-1507-3841, v. 1

Abbry Security Company, LLC a subsidiary of First Community Development  Corporation of New Jersey Inc.  MUNICIPAL COURT SECURITY CONTRACT  A   contract   by   and   between   Abbry   Security   Company,   LLC   a   subsidiary   of   First   Community  Development Corporation of New Jersey having its principal place of business located at  187 Cortlandt Street, Belleville, New Jersey 07109, hereinafter referred to as the contractor,  AND  The   Township   of   West   Orange,   a   Municipal   Corporation   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   having   its  principal   place   of   business   located   at   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   07052,  hereinafter referred to as the township,  Section 1  It   is   the   intention   of   Abbry   Security   to   help   create   jobs   and   aid   in   the   support   of   individuals   that  are disabled & disadvantaged.  The   Township   of   West   Orange   has   developed   Courtroom   Security   Procedures   that   are   in   place  to   protect   the   Municipal   Courthouse,   employees   of   the   court   as   well   as   citizens   involved   with  various courtroom proceedings.  Section   2 -   Work to Be Performed  Abbry   Security   Company   LLC,   will   provide   municipal   court   security   in   accordance   with   the  original   specifications   that   are   on   file   which   shall   include   but   not   be   limited   to,   security   screening  of   all   visitors,   use   of   magnetometer   and   procedures   and   hand   held   metal   detection   processes,  monitoring   of   the   courthouse   and   courtroom,   as   well   as   other   measures   to   keep   the   courtroom  and visitors safe.  Section   3   -Security Guards  Abbry   Security   Company   LLC,   will   provide   an   appropriate   number   of   unarmed   security   guards  to   provide   said   security   services   in   accordance   with   and   under   the   direction   of   the   Court  Administrator.  Section   4 -   Scope of Services:  Abbry   Security   LLC   will   provide   said   services   in   line   with   the   West   Orange   Police   Department  “ Security   Screening   Procedures ”   dated   October   23,   2007.   Any   deviation   from   the  aforementioned   procedures   must   be   requested   in   writing   and   approved   by   the   West   Orange  Police   Department   prior   to   deviation.   Abbry   shall   follow   all   procedures   established   for   Court  Security   by   the   West   Orange   Police   Department   as   currently   in   existence   and   as   may   be  amended.  Section   5 -   Ability to Perform  Abbry   will   consider   the   Township's   best   interests   as   its   own.   If,   at   any   time,   Abbry   considers   its  ability   to   render   the   best   service   compromised   in   any   way,   it   will   promptly   bring   such   concerns  to the Township's attention so that the parties' relationship will be maintained.

Section   6 -   Quality Control  Abbry   represents   and   warrants   that   it   has   established   policies   for   quality   control   which   involved  the   highest   levels   of   its   management.   Visits   and   inspections   by   supervisors   and   or   managers  are routine, and follow up, where necessary, is immediate.  Section   7 -   Payment for Services  Payment   of   services   prescribed   in   the   proposal   dated   July   23,   2013   have   been   amended   to   an  hourly   rate   of   $15.00   per   hour   per   guard.   Payment   will   be   due   upon   Abbry's   submission   of   duly  authenticated   payment   vouchers,   as   approved   by   the   Township   of   West-Orange.   Any   failure   by  Abbry   to   remit   a   voucher   or   invoice   within   thirty   (30)   days   of   the   services   rendered   will   render  void the request for payment.  Section   8 -   Contract  This   proposal   constitutes   the   complete   Contract   between   the   parties.   The   Contract   will   remain   in  full   force   and   effect   for   two   (2)   years   upon   mutual   agreement   between   Abbry   and   the   Township  of   West   Orange.   All   renewals,   extensions,   and   modifications   must   be   in   writing   and   signed   by  both parties, and shall be annexed to this agreement.  Section   9 -   Insurance  Abbry   shall   provide   and   keep   in   force   General   Public   Liability   Insurance   with   the   Municipality  named   as   an   additional   insured,   and   Workers   Compensation   Insurance   coverage   -   both   in  amounts   satisfactory   to   the   Township,   but   in   no   event   shall   the   coverage   be   for   less   than  $1,000,000   per   claim   or   occurrence.   All   insurance   shall   cover   all   services   to   be   provided  hereunder for the term of this Contract and any extensions, renewals or modifications.  The   Township   of   West   Orange   agrees   to   enter   into   this   contract   for   services   with   Abbry  Security.   The   foregoing   terms   constitute   the   complete   Contract   between   the   parties,   together  with   any   subsequently   mutually   agreed   upon   modifications   which   shall   be   in   writing.   This  document defines the responsibilities of both parties.  The Municipality agrees that service commenced and will continue for the term specified herein.

IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   the   parties   hereto   have   executed   this   agreement   as   of   April   ___   ,  2016.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF, they have executed this Agreement.  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  Robert D. Parisi, Mayor   Date  Attest:  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Date  CONTRACTOR  Contractor Signature   Contractor Printed Name  Attest   Date  4817-6216-2481, v. 1

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING PURCHASE OF BODY WORN CAMERAS AND SUPPORTING EQUIPMENT LICENSE AND WARRA

pages.text: 119-16  May 3, 2016  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   purchase   body-  worn   cameras   for   use   by   the   West   Orange   Police   Department   (the   “ WOPD ” )   and   supporting  software licensing, maintenance and warranties ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   received   a t   least   three   (3)   price   quotes   consistent   with  the requirements of   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(4); and  WHEREAS,   the   price   quote   from   Watch   Guard,   a   vendor   with   a   state   contract,   for   the  purchase   of   sixty-five   (65)   body   worn   cameras   with   the   supporting   software   licenses,  maintenance   plans   and   warranties   provided   the   best   economic   value   for   the   Township ,   annexed  hereto as Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   purchase   of   this   equipment   from   a   state   contract   vendors   constitutes   an  exception   to   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(4) ,   a   copy   of   the  State Contract is annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ B ” ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   purchase   of   support   and   maintenance   of   proprietary   computer   software  constitutes   an   exception   to   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law   pursuant   to   N.JS.A.   40A:11-5(1)(dd);  and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   anticipates   that   the   purchase   of   this   equipment   shall   be   made  in   part   from   a   $30,000   grant   i ssued   by   the   State   of   New   Jersey   as   part   of   the   Body   Worn  Camera Assistance Program, annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ C . ”  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   Township   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   price   quote   received   from   Watch  Guard   and   purchase   the   body-worn   cameras   with   the   supporting   software   licensing,  maintenance   plan   and warranties ; and it is further

120-16  May 3, 2016  1  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law,   N.J.S.A.   40A:12A-1   et   seq.,  provides   a   mechanism   to   empower   and   assist   local   governments   in   efforts   to   promote   programs  of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   desires   to   explore   whether  the   real   properties   located   between   Central   Avenue   and   Mitchell   Street   and   more   commonly  known   as   Block   9,   Lots   11.01,   11.02,   11.03,   32,   and   36   on   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Tax  Map,   inclusive   of   any   and   all   streets,   “ paper ”   streets,   private   drives   and   right   of   ways   (the   “ Study  Area ” ) may be an appropriate area for consideration for the program of redevelopment;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Study   Area   currently   has   a   high   vacancy   rate   for   retail   stores   located  thereon and that has remained as such for an extended period of time; and  WHEREAS,   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law   sets   forth   a   specific   procedure  for establishing an area in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A .   40A:12A-6,   prior   to   the   Township   making   a  determination   as   to   whether   the   Study   Area   qualifies   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment,   the  Township   Council   must   authorize   the   Planning   Board,   by   resolution,   to   undertake   a   preliminary  investigation   to   determine   whether   the   Study   Area   meets   the   criteria   of   an   area   in   need   of  redevelopment set forth in   N.J.S.A . 40A:12A-5; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Council   wishes   to   direct   the   Township   of   West   Ornage  Planning   Board   to   undertake   such   preliminary   investigation   to   determine   whether   the   Study  Area   meets   the   criteria   for   designation   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   pursuant   to  N.J.S.A .   40A:12A-5   and   in   accordance   with   the   investigation   and   hearing   process   set   forth   at  N.J.S.A . 40A:12A-6

120-16  May 3, 2016  2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   that   the   Township   Council   hereby   directs   the  West   Orange   Township   Planning   Board   to   conduct   the   necessary   investigation   and   to   hold   a  public   hearing   to   determine   whether   the   Study   Area   defined   hereinabove   qualifies   for  designation   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment   under   the   criteria   and   pursuant   to   the   public  hearing process set forth in   N.J.S.A . 40A:12A-1,   et seq. ; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   redevelopment   area   determination   shall   further  authorize   the   municipality   to   use   all   those   powers   provided   by   the   New   Jersey   Legislature   for  use   in   a   redevelopment   area,   including   the   power   of   eminent   domain   (hereinafter   referred   to   as  “ Condemnation Redevelopment Area ” ).  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Planning   Board   shall   submit   its   findings   and  recommendations   to   the   Township   Council   in   the   form   of   a   Resolution   with   supportive  documentation.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   certified   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   forwarded   to   the  Township   Clerk,   Chief   Financial   Officer   and   Township   Planning   Board   and   Planning   Board  Secretary.  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen   J.   Carnevale , R.M.C.   Victor Cirilo  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   May 3, 2016  4829-7360-1329, v. 1

121-16  May 3, 2016  1  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   determined   that   there   has   been   or  suspects   that   there   has   been   a   discharge   of   hazardous   substances   or   a   hazardous   waste   on  the   Valley   Central   Corridor ,   Block   9 ,   Lot s   1,   7,   11.01,   11.02,   11.03,   32,   36,   44,   50,   and   56 ,  which borders on   Central Avenue   and   Mitchell Street   (the   “ Valley Central Corridor ” ) , and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   applying   from   the   Hazardous   Discharge  Site   Fund   for   funding   for   the   investigation   and   cleanup   of   the   Valley   Central   Corridor   in   order   to  determine the extent of any   hazardous substance or h azardous waste; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   intends   to   pursue   acqui sition   of   the   property  through   voluntary   conveyance   or   o ther   means   provided   by   law   for   purposes   of   remediation   and  redevelopment ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   will   require   professional   environmental  services   of   a   L icensed   S ite   R emediation   P rofessional   ( “ LSRP ” )   to   assist   the   Township   in   its  efforts regarding the investigation and cleanup of   the Valley Central Corridor ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   previously   retained   the   firm   Matrix   New  World   ( “ Matrix ” )   26   Columbia   Turnpike,   Florham   Park,   New   Jersey   for   professional  environmental services; and  WHEREAS ,   Matrix   had   submitted   a   response   to   the   Township ’ s   Request   for  Qualifications for Municipal Engineering Services; and  WHEREAS ,   Matrix   qualifies   to   contract   with   the   Township   under   the   requirements   set  forth under the New Jersey Pay-to-Play Statute; and  WHEREAS,   Matrix   has   submitted   a   proposal   and   fee   schedule   for   the:   (1)   Preparation   of  a   Preliminary   Investigation   Study;   (2)   Preparation   of   a   Redevelopment   Plan;   and   (3)   Application  for   the   Hazardous   Discharge   Remediation   Fund   Grant ,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A , ”   to  provide   the   Township   with   professional   environmental   services   regarding   the   investigation   and  cleanup of the Valley Central Corridor   pursuant to the terms and conditions contained therein .

121-16  May 3, 2016  2  NOW,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE   that   that   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   committed   to   the   redevelopment   of  the   Valley   Central   Corridor   and   finds   that   a   realistic   opportunity   exists   for   the   redevelopment   of  the   Valley   Central   Corridor   within   a   three   year   period   after   the   completion   of   the   remediation   of  this   site   either   through   a   planned   redevelopment   project,   or   through   alternate   redevelopment ,  and   be it   further  RESOLVED ,   that   Matrix   be   and   is   designated   the   Township ’ s   LSRP   for   the   investigation  and cleanup   of   the Central Valley Corridor ;   and be it further  RESOLVED ,   that   t he   Mayor   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   retain   Matrix   consistent   with  the proposal and fee schedule , annexed hereto as   Exhibit   “ A ” ;   and be it further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law. Matrix New World Engineering, PC 442 State Route 35, 2 nd   Floor Eatontown, NJ 07724 732.588.2999   F: 973.240.1818 www.matrixneworld.com   WBE/DBE/SBE  April 27, 2016  Via Email (admin@westorange.org)  Township of West Orange 66 Main Street West Orange, New Jersey 07052 Attn:   John K. Sayers, Business Administrator  RE:   CENTRAL AVENUE PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION STUDY, REDEVELOPMENT PLAN AND HDSRF GRANT APPLICATION BLOCK 9, LOTS 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, & 36 AND BLOCK 7, LOT 22 TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, ESSEX COUNTY, NEW JERSEY MATRIX NO. PLD16-201  Dear Mr. Sayers: Matrix New World Engineering, PC (Matrix) is pleased to submit this proposal for professional planning and environmental services for the preparation of a Preliminary Investigation to determine whether or not Block 9, Lots 11.01, 11.02, 11.03, 32, and 36 and Block 7, Lot 22 (Subject Property) would qualify as an Area in Need of Redevelopment (Preliminary Investigation Study).   Please note that based on the Township of West Orange’s Tax  Map dated March 9, 1986, the subject property also includes Block 9, Lots 8, 9 and 40. In addition, should the Subject Property qualify as an Area in Need of Redevelopment, a Scope Item for the preparation of a Redevelopment Plan is also included within this proposal. Further, this proposal includes the preparation of a grant application fo r the Subject Property under New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP)’s  Site Remediation Program Hazardous Discharge Site Remediation Fund (HDSRF) grant program. In addition, this proposal is intended to provide the basis for Matrix to become the Licensed Site Remediation Professional for the Subject Property. Our professionals have substantial experience in preparing Preliminary Investigation Redevelopment Studies, Redevelopment   Plans,   Rehabilitation   Plans   and   in   designing   all   aspects   of   redevelopment   projects   in municipalities located throughout the State of New Jersey in rural, suburban, and urban settings. Matrix also has extensive experience in providing environmental engineering services through which it has gained a detailed knowledge of West Orange. We will leverage our expertise and local knowledge of the Township of West Orange to complete the Scope of Services included herein. It is assumed that the Township will provide the following documentation to assist us in the preparation of the Preliminary Investigation Study, Redevelopment Plan and HDSRF grant application:     Any existing plans, reports, analyses, or other documentation pertaining to the Subject Property that is pertinent to the preparation of the items noted above.

PLD16-201  SCOPE OF SERVICES  ITEM 1:   PREPARATION OF A PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION STUDY   (Phase PL23)  We will attend an initial meeting with Township representatives to obtain any available existing documentation  pertaining to the Subject Property and discuss the Township’s goals and vision for the   Preliminary Investigation Study. Preliminary mapping and handouts will be provided at the meeting to aid in the discussion. We will prepare a Preliminary Investigation Study for the Subject Property in accordance with the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law (N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-1, et seq.). The Study will demonstrate whether or not the Subject Property qualifies as an Area in Need of Redevelopment. The Study will include mapping prepared using Matrix ’s   Geographic Information System (GIS) in order to illustrate the existing conditions and delineate the project area. Upon completion of the draft Preliminary Investigation Study, copies of the draft Plan will be provided to the Project Team for review and comment. Subsequently, the draft Preliminary Investigation Study will be updated to reflect the comments received and a final draft Preliminary Investigation Study will be provided to the Township for consideration for adoption. This proposal includes attendance at up to two (2) public meetings in support of the adoption of the Preliminary Investigation Study, including one (1) Planning Board hearing and one (1) Township Council meeting. We will present the Preliminary Investigation Study for adoption at the above-referenced meetings and be available to the Township Council and Planning Board during the meetings to provide advisory services regarding the Study and overall Redevelopment process. This Item does not include the preparation of any public notices, resolutions, or related documentation, as it is assumed that others will prepare all of the public notices, resolutions and related documentation.  FIXED FEE:   $5,200.00  ITEM 2:   PREPARATION OF A REDEVELOPMENT PLAN   (Phase PL23)  We will attend an initial meeting with Township representatives to obtain any available existing documentation  pertaining to the Subject Property and discuss the Township’s goals and vision for the   Redevelopment Plan (Plan). Preliminary mapping and handouts will be provided at the meeting to aid in the discussion. Subsequently, a draft Redevelopment Plan will be prepared based upon available documentation provided by the Township, applicable sections of the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law (N.J.S.A. 40A:12A-1, et seq.), a review of the Township ’s   Master Plan, Zoning Ordinance, and Tax Maps, and sound land use planning principles. The Plan will be designed to maximize the value of the Subject Property and may include additional parcels at the  Township’s discretion if it will   increase the viability and marketability of the Subject Property. The draft Plan will be designed to fully accommodate the   Township’s goals and vision for the   Subject Property. In order to minimize costs the draft Plan will be limited to the minimum amount of information required by the Local Redevelopment and Housing Law in order to provide a sound Redevelopment Plan that maximizes the value and marketability of the Subject Property. The information to be provided is as follows:     A project description     A   narrative regarding the Plan’s   relationship to definitive local objectives as to appropriate land uses, density of population and improved traffic and public transportation, public utilities, recreational and community facilities and other public improvements     Goals and objectives

PLD16-201     Zoning standards, including land uses, bulk, and area requirements     A Map depicting the Redevelopment Area boundary     A concise statement regarding the relationship of the Redevelopment Plan to the following documents:  o   The Master Plans of contiguous municipalities  o   Bergen County Master Plan  o   The New Jersey State Development and Redevelopment Plan It is important to note that, while this proposal does not include an update of the Zoning Map to depict the Redevelopment Area or the preparation of any Ordinances to amend the section of the Township ’s   Code that pertains to the Zoning Map, Matrix is available to provide these services for an additional fee as part of a separate proposal. Upon completion of the draft Redevelopment Plan, copies of the draft Plan will be provided to the Project Team for review and comment. Subsequently, the draft Redevelopment Plan will be updated to reflect the comments received and a final draft Redevelopment Plan will be provided to the Township for consideration for adoption. This proposal includes attendance at up to three (3) public meetings in support of the adoption of the Redevelopment Plan, including up to two (2) Township Council meetings and one (1) Planning Board hearing. We will present the Redevelopment Plan for adoption at the above-referenced meetings and be available to the Township Council and Planning Board during the meetings to provide advisory services regarding the Plan and overall Redevelopment process. This Item does not include the preparation of any public notices, resolutions, or related documentation, as it is assumed that others will prepare all of the public notices, resolutions and related documentation.  FIXED FEE:   $8,250.00  ITEM 3:   HAZARDOUS DISCHARGE SITE REMEDIATION FUND (HDSRF) GRANT APPLICATION   (Phase PL15)  We will prepare one (1) grant application to the NJDEP under the HDSRF Program for the Selecto Flash Property and other adjacent properties wit hin the overall subject property at the Client’s discretion   to be included within the same grant application. This item includes the preparation of required grant application forms, coordination with the Client and Project Team members for environmental and other technical information to list on and attach to the grant application, and submission of the grant application to NJDEP. While this proposal item does not include any services subsequent to the submission of the grant application referenced above, Matrix is available to provide additional services for an additional fee as part of a separate proposal. It is assumed that the required attachments for the grant application will be provided by others, if they are comprised of existing documentation, or prepared by Matrix as part of a separate proposal.  FIXED FEE:   $4,500.00  CLOSING  Any item listed above which is not ordered will not be billed.   Separate authorization from the client will be requested prior to commencing services outside the scope of this proposal. All out-of-pocket expenses including, but not limited to, application fees, laboratory testing costs, mylar copies, certified mailings, photographs, blueprints, and special deliveries are considered additional to the proposal items unless specifically noted within the scope of this proposal.

PLD16-201  The terms and conditions of this proposal are subject to the Matrix Terms and Conditions for Professional Services and Matrix Fee Schedule, as submitted to the Township previously.  PAYMENT SCHEDULE  Payment shall be in accordance with the Charges, Billing, and Payment schedule outlined in the Terms and Conditions for Professional Services document unless prior written arrangements have been made with Matrix.  Services shall commence upon the receipt of a signed purchase order or other appropriate written authorization from the Client.  We thank you for the opportunity to submit this proposal.   Please feel free to contact me directly with any questions   or   comments   regarding   the   scope   or   fees   as   indicated   at   (973)   585-5266   or   via   email   to pvandenkooy@matrixneworld.com. Sincerely,  Peter Van den Kooy, PP, AICP Planning Department Manager cc:   Rose DeLorenzo, CHMM, LSRP

2482-16  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   5 , SECTION S   8.1 ,   and 8.3   OF THE  REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( Food Truck Licensing )  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   5   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is to   revise the municipal code   section governing   licensing  for retail food establishments to incorporate licensing provisions for the operation of food trucks  within the Township of West Orange .  II.   CHAPTER   5  ,   SECTION   8.1   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING :  5 - 8.1   License Required .  It   shall   be   unlawful   for   any   person   or   any   body   to   conduct   a   retail   food   establishment   as  defined   and   governed   by   Chapter   XII   24   of   the   State   Sanitary   Code   of   New   Jersey   without  having   first   obtained   a   license   from   the   Department   of   Health   and   Welfare   or   without   complying  with   all   of   the   provisions   concerning   operation   and   maintenance   of   the   establishment   as  contained in the Code.  III.   CHAPTER   5,   SECTION   8  .  3   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING :  5 - 8 . 3   Fees .  The yearly license fees for retail food establishments shall be as follows:  a.   Bakery, Specialty: seventy-five ($75.00) dollars.  Bakery, Full Service: one hundred ($100.00) dollars.  b.   Candy Counters: twenty-five ($25.00) dollars each counter.  c.   Catering, Off Premises Only: one hundred twenty-five ($125.00) dollars.  d.   Delicatessen, Grocery, No Seating: fifty ($50.00) dollars.  Delicatessen, with Seating or Catering: seventy-five ($75.00) dollars.  e.   Frozen Dessert, No Seating: fifty ($50.00) dollars.  Frozen Dessert, Seating: seventy-five ($75.00) dollars.  f.   Health   Care   Facility,   with   Bed   Capacity   up   to   40:   one   hundred   twenty-five   ($125.00)  dollars.  Health Care Facility, with Bed Capacity over 40: three hundred ($300.00) dollars.  Health Care Facility Nonprofit: two hundred fifty ($250.00) dollars.

2  g.   Itinerant   Restaurant,   Carnival   or   Fair   with   Food:   twenty-five fifty   ($ 25  50  .00)   dollars   for  the   first   day   and   additional   ten twenty-five   ($ 10  25  .00)   dollars   for   each   day   thereafter up   to  five (5)   additional   days .  h.   Meat or Fish Market: seventy-five ($75.00) dollars.  i.   Mobile   Retail   Food   Establishment   –   Itinerant   Restaurant   Only:   fifty   ($50.00)   for   first   day  and additional twenty-five ($25.00)   dollars   for up   five (5) additional days.  j.   Mobile Truck, Packaged Goods Only: one hundred twenty-five ($125.00) dollars.  k j .   Packaged Goods Store: fifty ($50.00) dollars.  l k .   Plant and Commercial Cafeterias: one hundred twenty-five ($125.00) dollars.  m l .   Restaurant,   Luncheonette,   Hotel,   Country   Club   or   Banquet   House   with   a   Seating   Capacity  of 0 to 25 persons: seventy-five ($75.00) dollars.  n m .   Restaurant,   Luncheonette,   Hotel,   Country   Club   or   Banquet   House   with   a   Seating   Capacity  of 26 to 50 Persons: one hundred ($100.00) dollars.  o n .   Restaurant,   Luncheonette,   Hotel,   Country   Club   or   Banquet   House   with   a   Seating   Capacity  of 51 to 100 persons: one hundred seventy-five ($175.00) dollars.  p o .   Restaurant,   Luncheonette,   Hotel,   Country   Club   or   Banquet   House   with   a   Seating   Capacity  of 101 to 200 Persons: two hundred seventy-five ($275.00) dollars.  q p .   Restaurant,   Luncheonette,   Hotel,   Country   Club   or   Banquet   House   with   a   Seating   Capacity  of Over 200 Persons: four hundred ($400.00) dollars.  r q .   Schools   and   Nursery   Schools   with   Food:   Profit:   seventy-five   ($75.00)   dollars.   Nonprofit:  fifty ($50.00) dollars.  s r .   Snack Bar: fifty ($50.00) dollars.  t s .   Supermarket with up to 20,000 square feet: one hundred fifty ($150.00) dollars.  Supermarket with over 20,000 square feet: two hundred fifty ($250.00) dollars.  u t .   Tavern or Liquor Store without Food Service: fifty ($50.00) dollars.  IV.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  V .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   the  invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.

3  V I.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C. 4  Legislative History  This   ordinance   is   drafted   to   expand   the   provisions   of   the   Municipal   Code   that   govern   licensing  for retail food establishment to incorporate licensing for food trucks operating within the Township .  4849-5025-0288, v. 1

2483-16  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   4 , SECTION   4.2   OF THE REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  (NOISE)  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   4   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   ordinance   is   to   revise   the   municipal   code   section   governing   excessive  noise   to   incorporate   and   exclude   on   Sundays   any   construction,   repairs   and/or   alterations   which  require a new certificate of occupancy within the Township of West Orange.  II.   CHAPTER   4,   SECTION   4.2   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING :  The   following   activities   are   hereby   declared   to   be   examples   of   noises   which   are   subject   to   the  provisions of this section:  a.   Radios;   Television;   Phonographs.   The   using,   operating,   or   permitting   to   be   played,   used  or   operated,   of   any   radio   receiving   set,   television,   musical   instrument,   phonograph   or  other   machine   or   device   for   the   producing   or   reproducing   of   sound   in   such   manner   as   to  disturb   the   health   or   comfort   of   neighboring   inhabitants   or   with   louder   volume   than   is  necessary   for   convenient   hearing   by   the   person   or   persons   who   are   in   the   room,   vehicle   or  chamber   in   which   such   machine   or   device   is   operated   and   who   are   voluntary   listeners.   The  operation   of   any   such   set,   instrument,   phonograph,   machine   or   device   between   the   hours  of   11:00   p.m.   and   7:00   a.m.   in   such   manner   as   to   be   plainly   audible   at   a   distance   of  twenty-five   (25)   feet   from   the   building,   structure   or   vehicle   in   which   it   is   located   shall   be  prima facie evidence of a violation of this section.  b.   Loudspeakers   for   Advertising.   The   using,   operating   or   permitting   to   be   played,   used   or  operated,   of   any   radio   receiving   set,   musical   instrument,   phonograph   or   other   machine   or  device   for   the   producing   or   reproducing   of   sound   which   is   cast   upon   the   public   streets   for  the purpose of commercial advertising.  c.   Yelling;   Shouting.   Yelling,   shouting,   hooting,   whistling   or   singing   on   the   public   streets  between   the   hours   of   11:00   p.m.   and   7:00   a.m.   so   as   to   disturb   the   comfort   or   repose   of  persons in any residential dwelling.  d.   Exhausts.   The   discharge   into   the   open   air   of   the   exhaust   of   any   steam   engine,   stationary  internal   combustion   engine,   motor   vehicle   or   the   internal   combustion   engine   of   a   leaf  blower,   power   hedge   trimmer   or   power   lawn   mower,   except   through   a   properly  functioning   muffler   or   other   device   which   will   effectively   prevent   explosive   or  excessively loud noises therefrom.  e.   Loading;   Unloading;   Emptying   Containers.   The   creation   of   loud   and   disturbing   noise   in  connection   with   loading   or   unloading   any   vehicle   or   the   opening,   emptying   or   destroying  of   bales,   boxes,   crates,   garbage   cans   or   other   containers   between   the   hours   of   9:00   p.m.  and 6:30 a.m.  f.   Construction;   Repair;   Maintenance.   Unless   otherwise   allowed   for   herein   excavation,  demolition,   construction,   repair   or   alteration   work   ("construction   activities")   shall   only   be  permitted   at   the   site   of   any   residential   or   commercial   property   or   on   any   vacant   land   other

2483-16  than   between   the   hours   of   7:30   a.m.   and   7:00   p.m.   from   Monday   through   Friday   or  between   the   hours   of   8:00   a.m.   and   7:00   p.m.   on   Saturday.   In   addition,   no   heavy  equipment   related   to   construction   activities   shall   be   permitted   to   arrive   at   or   depart   from  any   residential   or   commercial   property   or   any   vacant   land   prior   to   7:30   a.m.   or   after   7:00  p.m.   Monday   through   Friday   and   prior   to   8:00   a.m.   or   after   5:00   p.m.   on   Saturdays   and  prior   to   9:00   a.m.   or   after   5:00   p.m.   on   Sundays.   Further,   no   persons,   other   than   security  personnel,   are   permitted   to   be   at   the   site   of   any   residential   or   commercial   property   or   on  any   vacant   land   during   the   hours   when   delivery,   removal   or   operation   of   heavy   equipment  is   prohibited.   Notwithstanding   the   foregoing,   the   owner   or   occupant   of   a   single-,   two-   or  three-family   residential   dwelling   (but   expressly   excluding   (i)   the   construction   of   a   new  residence   or   a   development ;   or   (ii)   construction,   repairs   and/or   alterations   which   require   a  new   certificate   of   occupancy )   can   engage   in   construction,   repairs   and/or   alterations   to  their residence on Sundays between 9:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m.  In   addition,   no   person   shall   operate   pile   driver,   steam   shovel,   bulldozer   or   other   earth   moving  machinery,   pneumatic   hammer,   hammer,   derrick,   steam   or   electric   hoist   or   similar   machinery  and   equipment   at   any   residential   or   commercial   property   or   any   vacant   land   except   between   the  hours   of   7:30   a.m.   and   7:00   p.m.   Monday   through   Friday   or   between   the   hours   of   8:00   a.m.   and  7:00 p.m. on Saturday.  Moreover,   no   person   shall   operate   a   leaf   blower,   chain   saw,   power   saw,   power   hedge   trimmer  or   power   lawn   mower   except   in   accordance   with   the   following   schedule   for   hours   and   days   of  use:  1.   On weekdays between 7:30 a.m. and 7:00 p.m.;  2.   On   Saturdays   between   8:00   a.m.   and   5:00   p.m.,   except   that   when   used   by   an   occupant  or owner of the premises between 8:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m.;  3.   On   Sundays   between   9:00   a.m.   and   5:00   p.m.,   except   that   when   used   by   an   occupant  or owner of the premises between 9:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m.  g.   The provisions of Ordinance 2156-07 *   shall apply in full force and effect.  h.   Notwithstanding   the   provisions   herein,   any   emergency   repair   may   occur   which   is   required  for   the   health,   safety   or   habitability   of   the   property   as   may   be   determined   by   the  Construction Official.  i.   Noise   Near   Schools,   Courts,   Churches   or   Hospitals.   The   creation   of   any   excessive   noise  on   any   street   adjacent   to   any   school,   institution   of   learning,   church,   court   or   hospital,  while   such   institution   is   in   use   and   which   interferes   with   the   working   of   the   institution   or  which   disturbs   or   unduly   annoys   patients   in   the   hospital   provided   that   conspicuous   signs  are   displayed   in   such   streets   indicating   that   the   same   is   a   quiet   area   because   of   the  presence of a school, church, court or hospital.  j.   Air-Conditioning   or   Air-Cooling   units.   The   operation   and   use   of   any   air-conditioning   or  air-cooling   units   or   apparatus,   including   all   noise   continuous   enough   to   be   measured   by   a  sound   level   meter,   devices   or   appurtenances,   but   not   limited   to   air-conditioning   and   air-  cooling   units,   fans,   blowers,   and   compressors,   pool   filters,   machinery   noise   from   industry,  during   the   hours   between   11:00   p.m.   and   7:30   a.m.,   creating   an   audible   noise   which  annoys   or   disturbs   the   quiet,   comfort   or   repose   of   persons   in   any   dwelling,   hotel,  apartment   house   or   other   type   of   residence   located   within   a   R-1,   R-2,   R-3,   R-4,   R-5,   R-6,  R-G   or   R-M   Zone   as   defined   in   Chapter   XXV,   Land   Use   Regulations,   shall   be   deemed   to  be   a   nuisance   and   unlawful.   Audible   noise   shall   mean   a   noise   intensity   of   over   forty   (40)  decibels   measured   by   a   noise   intensity   measuring   meter   in   the   center   of   the   room   or   place

2483-16  wherein   the   annoyance   exists.   The   sound   level   meter   shall   conform   to   the   applicable  standard of the American Standard Institution, Incorporation, New York, New York.  k.   Unreasonable   Noise.   Any   excessive   or   unusually   loud   sound   which   either   annoys,  disturbs,   injures   or   endangers   the   comfort,   repose,   health,   peace   or   safety   of   a   reasonable  person of normal sensitivities.  IV.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  V.   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   the  invalidity thereof shall not affect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  VI.   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   upon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  ________________________________  Victor Cirilo  Council President  ________________________________  Honorable Robert D. Parisi  Mayor  Introduced:  Adopted:  Approved as to form on the basis of the facts provided:  ______________________________  Richard D. Trenk  Township Attorney

2483-16  Legislative History  This   ordinance   is   drafted   to   expand   the   provisions   of   the   Municipal   Code   governing  excessive   noise   to   incorporate   and   exclude   on   Sundays   construction,   repairs   and/or   alterations  which require a new certificate of occupancy within the Township of West Orange.  4826-7402-9873, v. 1
